records.ety: [Pref. con- + L. centrum center. Cf. Concenter.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Concentrated; p. pr. & vb. n. Concentrating.]

defs: To bring to, or direct toward, a common center; to unite more closely; to gather into one body, mass, or force; to fix; as, to concentrate rays of light into a focus; to concentrate the attention.
quotes: (He) concentrated whole force at his own camp.

defs: To increase the strength and diminish the bulk of, as of a liquid or an ore; to intensify, by getting rid of useless material; to condense; as, to concentrate acid by evaporation; to concentrate by washing; -- opposed to dilute.
quotes: Spirit of vinegar concentrated and reduced to its greatest strength.

defs: To approach or meet in a common center; to consolidate; as, population tends to concentrate in cities.
